checks.detail: instruction.md, task.toml, environment/Dockerfile, and tests/test.sh all present.
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml are all present.
allow_internet=false, agent.timeout_sec=7200, verifier.timeout_sec=600, build_timeout_sec=600, agent.user=agent.
Dockerfile installs both git and tmux via apt-get.
tests/ contains extra assets (compute_reward.py, harness.py, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, visible_cases/) that should live in environment/tests/.
reasons: compute_reward.py is at tests/ root instead of being bundled via Dockerfile COPY
harness.py is at tests/ root
hidden_cases/ directory is at tests/ root
perf_data/ directory is at tests/ root
visible_cases/ directory is at tests/ root

checks.detail: No stray files at task root after ignoring gitignored and standard-ignored entries.

notes: Move tests/compute_reward.py, tests/harness.py, tests/hidden_cases/, tests/perf_data/, and tests/visible_cases/ to environment/tests/ and COPY them into /tests via the Dockerfile so tests/ contains only test.sh.

checks.detail: task.toml sets [agent] user = "agent".
All required isolation primitives are present in the Dockerfile.
reasons: present: useradd agent, chown -R agent:agent /app, chown agent:agent /solution, mkdir -p /tests && chmod 700 /tests, strace installed (with git/tmux), gcc/make for verifier compilation, cmark reference installed root-only under /opt/verifier_tools (chmod -R go-rwx)

checks.detail: test.sh locks /logs/verifier first, wipes, sanitises env, runs agent code as agent under strace+timeout, scores outside strace, always exits 0.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier BEFORE rm -rf, env sanitisation (unsets LD_PRELOAD/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PYTHONPATH/etc, fixed PATH), /tests re-locked to 0700, pkill -u agent before snapshot, build+correctness+perf runs use `su agent` inside `strace -f -e trace=... -o <log> timeout N`, reward_tamper_in scans each strace log, converter_execve_in tripwire, compute_reward.py and harness.py grade invoked OUTSIDE strace as root, fail_with always exits 0, final exit 0, no `set -euo pipefail`, fallback reward 0.0 via compute_reward.py --fail

checks.detail: runs_agent_code: verifier compiles and executes the agent's C md2html binary, so su-agent + strace wrapping is required and present.
Pure C task with no Python/Node package-manager dependencies, so no lockfile is applicable.
No Python or Node package manager is used; Dockerfile only invokes apt-get and builds cmark from source.
task.toml does not set `docker_image`, which is expected during development but must be set before final delivery.
reasons: task.toml has no [environment].docker_image field

notes: Before final delivery, build and push the image to a registry and set [environment].docker_image in task.toml.
Consider pinning the cmark git clone to a specific commit SHA instead of --depth 1 on the default branch for stronger reproducibility.

checks.detail: Deliverable (/app/workspace/md2html), interface (stdin/stdout), scope, and build commands are explicit and unambiguous.
Final section states 'You have 2 hours. Build, test, and iterate.'
Reads more like a formal spec with many headings than a conversational colleague handoff.
reasons: Heavy use of section headers (Overview, Compilation, Interface, Goals, Workspace, Time Limit)
Bulleted spec-style enumerations dominate over prose

checks.detail: 116 lines — slightly over 100 but acceptable for a parser task that needs scope enumeration.
No mention of scorer, verifier, reward, or evaluation; 'tests' refers only to the visible dev test corpus.
make, gcc, python3, and timeout are shown in example commands; starter Makefile and md2html.c are provided in the workspace.

notes: Consider trimming section headers and converting to a more conversational handoff tone to better match colleague-handoff style.

checks.detail: Stub md2html emits empty string so all cases fail; performance is gated by correctness so composite collapses to 0.
Reward file is locked+wiped before any agent code runs, /tests is re-locked to 0700, builds/runs occur as agent under strace, scoring runs as root outside strace from CLI counters only.
Every md2html build (make/gcc) and run (correctness + perf) is invoked via `su agent -s /bin/bash -c 'env -i ...'`.
strace -f wraps build, correctness, and each perf run; reward_tamper_in() and converter_execve_in() are checked before scoring.
compute_reward.py runs as `python3 -ISs`, takes only CLI counters, and never imports or reads from /app or any agent-writable path.
fail_with() always writes reward 0.0 and exits 0; build/correctness/perf invocations are bounded by `timeout`, and test.sh ends with `exit 0`.
Byte-exact HTML match per case; starter/empty/regressing outputs yield 0 correctness, and performance is multiplied by correctness so a fast-but-wrong binary collapses to 0.
Scoring compares output to CommonMark spec HTML byte-for-byte, so any correct from-scratch C parser scores fully regardless of internal algorithm.
Two dims (correctness, performance) are aggregated as weighted_sum but performance is multiplicatively gated by correctness_rate, so neither dim is trivially maxable without the other.
Empty output, identity echo, or random text all fail byte-exact comparison against expected HTML, yielding ~0 correctness and therefore ~0 composite.
compute_reward.py only consumes CLI counters and an optional failed-cases list; harness.py does grading independently and neither imports agent code, so scoring is re-runnable from staged outputs.
Both success and failure paths emit score (float), subscores (list of {subtask, score}), and additional_data; reward.txt mirrors the float.
Verifier scores correctness (visible+hidden CommonMark cases) and performance vs cmark, both aligned with instruction's stated goals of correct + fast.
Instruction links CommonMark 0.31.2 spec and enumerates the 9 leaf-block sections; internal scoring thresholds (5x cmark, correctness gate) need not be disclosed.
gcc/make installed via Dockerfile, starter Makefile and md2html.c present in /app/workspace; solve.sh uses the stock Makefile path which the verifier builds.
solution/solve.sh exists (63 lines) and installs a token-gated cmark-wrapper md2html.c.
All 54 hidden cases cover the same 9 CommonMark sections enumerated in instruction.md (Tabs, Thematic breaks, ATX/Setext headings, Indented/Fenced code, HTML blocks, Paragraphs, Blank lines).

Slim base, apt lists cleaned, cmake purged after building cmark; every remaining package (build-essential/gcc/make for agent C build, git/tmux/strace as Harbor hard-rule tools, procps for pkill in test.sh, file for the ELF check, ca-certificates for the cmark git clone) is load-bearing.
Only solve.sh present in /solution.
Most sections are load-bearing but a few overlap with isolation already in place.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 + wipe of /logs/verifier (mandatory)
useful: strace -f around as-agent build and md2html runs (catches forks, reward writes, external converter execve)
useful: env sanitization (LD_PRELOAD/PYTHONPATH/CC) for the root verifier process
useful: static cheat scan as a fast-fail before expensive strace runs
useful: ELF check via `file` to reject script wrappers
useful: FD-pinned grading in harness.py (O_NOFOLLOW, nlink>1, regular-file checks)
redundant: `chmod -R go-rx /tests/visible_cases /tests/hidden_cases /tests/perf_data` is dominated by `chmod 700 /tests` (no-traverse means inner perms unreachable)
redundant: `pkill -u agent` before strace phase is largely subsumed by strace -f waiting for all descendants (kept as belt-and-suspenders)

checks.detail: No individual file exceeds 30MB; largest assets are small JSON test cases and ~4.5MB perf corpus.

Heavy test assets live under `tests/` at task root instead of `environment/tests/`, so ~5MB (harness.py + compute_reward.py + hidden_cases/ + perf_data/ + visible_cases/) is uploaded per trial via docker compose cp rather than baked into the image.
reasons: tests/ contains harness.py, compute_reward.py, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, visible_cases/ — Harbor convention is that only test.sh sits at the task root; everything else should be in environment/tests/ and COPYed to /tests at build time

environment/test-suite/ duplicates tests/visible_cases/ (same 117 JSONs) — this duplication is intentional (agent-visible copy in /app/test-suite vs verifier copy in locked /tests), so not waste, but worth knowing
no .dockerignore in environment/ — minor

notes: Move harness.py, compute_reward.py, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, visible_cases/ from tests/ into environment/tests/ and COPY them into /tests (chmod 700) in the Dockerfile; leave only test.sh in tests/.
Drop the redundant chmod -R go-rx on /tests subdirs in test.sh (dominated by /tests 0700).

instruction_quality.task_name: CommonMark leaf blocks converter in C
instruction_quality.task_summary: Implement a Markdown-to-HTML converter in C from scratch covering CommonMark leaf blocks (headings, code blocks, HTML blocks, thematic breaks, paragraphs). Reads stdin, writes HTML to stdout, must match the CommonMark reference output.

measures: /logs/verifier chmod 700 + rm -rf before any agent execution
/tests re-locked to 0700 with go-rx stripped from visible/hidden/perf subdirs
cmark reference kept in /opt/verifier_tools mode go-rwx (root-only)
output staging dir root-owned 0755, per-case files 0666 only
FD-pinned O_NOFOLLOW reads with S_ISREG + nlink<=1 checks in harness.grade
Agent user (uid 1000) owns /app only
All compiles and md2html invocations run via `su agent -s /bin/bash -c 'env -i PATH=... ...'`
Scoring runs as root, OUTSIDE strace, with python3 -ISs
strace -f -e clone,clone3,fork,vfork,execve,openat over build, correctness, and per-perf-file runs
reward_tamper_in greps for openat of reward.{txt,json} with O_WRONLY|O_RDWR|O_CREAT
converter_execve_in greps for execve of cmark/pandoc/markdown/python/node/perl/ruby in md2html run logs
pkill -9 -u agent before workspace snapshot, with retry+abort if survivors
Each su agent block runs under timeout (300/480/60s) so descendants cannot stall the verifier
compute_reward.py only reads CLI integers and an optional root-written failed-cases file under /logs/verifier (0700)
reward.json/reward.txt written atomically via os.replace
Performance is multiplicatively gated by correctness so fast-empty binaries score ~0
EXPECTED_ORACLE_FLAG (md2html-leaf-blocks-oracle-ae01f875ea572bb4) is hardcoded in root-only /tests/test.sh and injected only into the oracle's env via [solution.env]
Anti-cheat skip and cmark unlock require both file presence AND byte-equal token match
unset LD_PRELOAD/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PYTHONPATH/PYTHONHOME/BASH_ENV/ENV/CFLAGS/CC/CXX/MAKEFLAGS at verifier entry
env -i used inside every su agent invocation with a fixed PATH
Static scan rejects #include of known MD libs, exec/system/popen of external converters, -lcmark-style linker flags, and wrapper scripts md2html.{py,js,sh,rb,pl}
ELF sanity check via `file` rejects shell/python/ascii-text masquerading as the converter

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Performance ratio can be inflated by fast-exiting on perf inputs (perf only measures wall time, not output)
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Performance is multiplicatively gated by correctness (perf_rate = raw * correctness_rate), so a fast-but-empty converter still scores ~0 overall; documented in compute_reward.py.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent could statically link or vendor a renamed Markdown library to bypass the #include-based static scan
acknowledged_vectors.reason: This requires actually producing CommonMark-correct output, which is the legitimate task; the static scan targets the obvious exec/system/popen/wrapper-script cheats, not all possible reuse, and any external-converter execve is independently caught by strace at run time.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent code under /app is not protected from runtime self-modification between snapshot and grading
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Snapshot is for audit only; grading reads pre-created root-owned output files (not /app), and the converter is rebuilt-from-source as the agent user under strace, so workspace tampering cannot change what the verifier scores.

reward_design.formula: composite = 0.5 * correctness_rate + 0.5 * (perf_rate_raw * correctness_rate), where correctness_rate = passed/(visible+hidden) and perf_rate_raw = mean over perf files of min(1, (cmark_median*5)/agent_median)
reward_design.formula_explanation: Equal weighting of correctness and speed, with performance multiplicatively gated by correctness so a fast-but-incorrect binary cannot harvest the perf half.

components.name: correctness
components.metric: fraction of 171 visible+hidden cases whose md2html output byte-matches the CommonMark reference HTML

components.name: performance
components.metric: mean over 2 perf inputs of min(1, 5*cmark_median_time / agent_median_time), median-of-3

dimension_analysis.name: correctness
dimension_analysis.notes: Byte-exact match across 171 CommonMark leaf-block cases including tricky tab/HTML-block/setext edges.

dimension_analysis.name: performance
dimension_analysis.notes: 5x cmark slack makes the perf ceiling reachable for a competent C parser; gated by correctness so cannot be gamed in isolation.

redundant_items.why: /tests is already chmod 700 root, so the agent cannot traverse into these subdirs regardless of their inner mode bits

redundant_items.item: harness.py, compute_reward.py, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, visible_cases/ live at task root tests/
redundant_items.why: Harbor uploads tests/ per trial via docker compose cp; bundling these into the image (move to environment/tests/ and COPY to /tests with chmod 700) eliminates ~5MB of per-trial upload and keeps the convention that tests/ at root only holds test.sh
Minor — there are no large/unwanted files in the build context today, but a .dockerignore documents intent and protects against future drift
